entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary cereal crop targeted for genetic improvement and production increase across multiple continents

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which leads research and germplasm distribution

entities.name: NARS
entities.description: National Agricultural Research Systems that partner with ICRISAT for breeding and varietal development

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A geographic region where ICRISAT and NARS collaborate on sorghum research

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A geographic region where ICRISAT and NARS collaborate on sorghum research

entities.name: LATIN AMERICA
entities.description: A geographic region where ICRISAT and NARS collaborate on sorghum research

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The scientific process of improving the genetic traits of sorghum to increase productivity and stability

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The genetic material of sorghum used as a basis for breeding and developing new varieties

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Organized efforts by NARS and ICRISAT to create improved sorghum cultivars

entities.name: CULTIVARS
entities.description: Specific varieties of sorghum developed through breeding and released for farmer use

entities.name: YIELDS
entities.description: The measure of sorghum production per unit area, used as a metric for research success

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The ability of sorghum varieties to maintain consistent production across different environments

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The ultimate goal of increasing sorghum production to ensure reliable access to food

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: The professional field and method used to develop improved crop varieties

entities.name: AGRO-ECOLOGICAL ZONES
entities.description: Geographic areas characterized by specific environmental conditions affecting sorghum growth
